title: EligibilityProof
description: Reduced, signed eligibility proof for affordability and subsidy decisioning. This schema enforces attribute-proof handling, prohibits raw financial evidence, and keeps sensitive SDOH proxy attributes such as eligibility status and service-area geography inside a field-level encrypted payload.

proof_id.description: Stable identifier for this reduced proof record.

patient_pseudo_id.description: Vault-issued pseudonymous patient identifier. No direct identity values are allowed in this document.

verification_provider_id.description: Internal registry identifier for the trusted verification provider or isolated verification service.

issuer.description: Issuer identifier embedded in the signed proof and mapped to an approved trust registry entry.

verification_method.description: How raw evidence was verified before reduction.

income_bracket_code.description: Reduced income classification code. Raw income amounts are intentionally not represented here.

affordability_tier.description: Tier-level affordability outcome derived from verified attributes.

verification_assurance_level.description: Verifier assurance rating for the reduced proof.

source_type.description: Normalized source category used for auditability without storing raw source artifacts.

proof_confidence.description: Optional verifier confidence score for downstream risk policies.

manual_review_required.description: Indicates that operational handling may require manual follow-up, without embedding raw review notes.

revocation_endpoint.description: Optional online revocation or status endpoint exposed by the verifier.

consent_reference_id.description: Reference to the consent or notice record captured before verification.

verified_at.description: Timestamp when the verification provider completed validation and proof issuance.

Digest.description: Canonical digest of the reduced proof payload or protected payload as defined by the issuer.

attribute_proof_only.description: Must always be true. The proof contains only reduced attributes, never the underlying evidence files.

contains_raw_financial_evidence.description: Must always be false. Raw pay stubs, tax files, benefits letters, statements, and similar evidence are prohibited from this schema.

contains_direct_identity.description: Must always be false. Direct identifiers such as name, SSN, MRN, street address, or DOB are not allowed.

contains_zip5.description: Must always be false. ZIP5 may be used transiently upstream but cannot persist in this proof.

algorithm.description: Approved signing algorithms for proof issuance. ES256 is preferred for FIPS-aligned deployments; RS256 is allowed for compatibility.

key_id.description: Versioned issuer signing key identifier from the trust registry.

encryption_algorithm.description: Field-level authenticated encryption for sensitive SDOH proxy and benefits-status attributes.

key_id.description: KMS/HSM-backed key identifier used to protect the payload.

aad_hash.description: Optional digest of external associated data bound to this ciphertext.

protected_attribute_names.description: Declares which sensitive attributes are sealed inside the encrypted payload.

EncryptedProtectedAttributes.description: Encrypted payload for sensitive reduced attributes. The decrypted JSON must conform to #/$defs/ProtectedAttributesPlaintext and must never be duplicated in plaintext elsewhere in the proof.

eligibility_status_normalized.description: Normalized eligibility outcome only. Named program details should not be exposed unless separately justified and encrypted.

service_area_code.description: Preferred minimized geography proxy. Use instead of ZIP-derived data when available.

zip3.description: Allowed only when a ZIP-derived proxy is operationally required. ZIP5 is not permitted.

household_size_band.description: Optional reduced household-size band. Exact household rosters are not permitted.

source_program_code.description: Optional encrypted source-program code when operationally required. Keep normalized and access-restricted.

ProtectedAttributesPlaintext.description: Canonical plaintext model of the sensitive reduced attributes before field-level encryption. This definition is provided so issuers and consumers share the same logical contract even though the values must be sealed at rest and in transit.
